body:
### Summary

This PR improves Apollon-based drag-and-drop quiz generation in Artemis. Instructors can now mark top-level diagram elements as well as nested class diagram elements, such as attributes and methods, as quiz-relevant. The generated drag-and-drop question uses more accurate cutouts and background placeholders based on the selected Apollon elements.

This PR also bumps the Apollon library dependency to `@tumaet/apollon@4.4.0`, which includes:

- **Assessment popover fix:** Clicking on diagram elements in assessment mode now correctly opens the feedback popup. Previously, tutors and instructors were completely unable to enter scores or feedback on any modeling element.
- **Real-time collaboration:** The new version introduces live collaborative editing with visible cursors, per-user presence indicators, and server-backed diagram version history with named snapshots that can be browsed and restored.

### Checklist

#### General

- [x] This is a small issue that I tested locally and was confirmed by another developer on a test server.
- [x] Language: I followed the [guidelines for inclusive, diversity-sensitive, and appreciative language](https://docs.artemis.tum.de/developer/guidelines/language).
- [x] I chose a title conforming to the [naming conventions for pull requests](https://docs.artemis.tum.de/developer/development-process#pr-naming-conventions).

#### Client

- [x] **Important**: I implemented the changes with a very good performance, prevented too many unnecessary REST calls and made sure the UI is responsive.
- [x] I **strictly** followed the principle of **data economy** for all client-server REST calls.
- [x] I **strictly** followed the [client coding guidelines](https://docs.artemis.tum.de/developer/guidelines/client-development).
- [x] I **strictly** followed the [AET UI-UX guidelines](https://ls1intum.github.io/ui-ux-guidelines/).
- [x] Following the [theming guidelines](https://docs.artemis.tum.de/developer/guidelines/client-theming), I did not introduce new Artemis-side hardcoded colors.
- [x] I added multiple screenshots/screencasts of my UI changes.

### Motivation and Context

Apollon drag-and-drop quiz generation previously did not fully support the new Apollon interactive selection flow. Instructors could mark quiz-relevant diagram elements, but Artemis did not reliably handle all selected element types.

In particular, nested class diagram elements such as attributes and methods were not treated as valid quiz-relevant elements, and generated cutouts/background placeholders could become inaccurate. This PR aligns Artemis with the new Apollon interactive selection model and restores reliable drag-and-drop quiz generation.

### Description

This PR updates the Artemis Apollon quiz generation flow to support selected Apollon elements more accurately.

The changes include:

- Recognize nested Apollon node elements, such as class attributes and methods, as quiz-relevant elements.
- Generate drag items and drop locations for nested selected elements.
- Crop generated SVG/PNG drag items to the selected SVG content instead of using oversized parent bounds.
- Keep the diagram background aligned with the full diagram while removing selected elements from the background.
- Preserve the existing top-level node and relationship generation behavior.
- Keep quiz generation compatible with Apollon's `interactive.elements` and `interactive.relationships` serialization.
- Keep the quiz dialog editor in editable modeling mode while exposing Apollon's interactive selection view.

No automated tests were added because the change depends heavily on SVG export/cropping behavior and manual Apollon editor interaction. Manual test steps are provided below.

### Steps for Testing

Prerequisites:
- 1 Instructor
- 1 Course with quiz exercise management access

1. Log in to Artemis as an instructor.
2. Navigate to Course Administration.
3. Open or create a quiz exercise.
4. Create or import an Apollon diagram for drag-and-drop quiz generation.
5. Open the Apollon diagram detail view.
6. Create a class diagram with at least two classes, attributes, methods, and one relationship.
7. Switch to the Apollon interactive selection view.
8. Select a full class node.
9. Select a class attribute or method.
10. Select a relationship.
11. Verify that "Generate Quiz Exercise" becomes enabled.
12. Generate the drag-and-drop quiz exercise.
13. Verify that generated drag items match the selected diagram parts.
14. Verify that drop locations appear at the correct positions on the background.
15. Verify that the background keeps the full diagram size and does not shift selected placeholders.
16. Repeat with a different diagram type and different selected elements to ensure stale selections are not reused.

#### Exam Mode Testing

This PR only affects quiz exercise creation/editing for instructors and Apollon diagram based drag-and-drop generation. Student quiz participation and exam mode rendering are not changed.

### Testserver States

To be filled after deployment/testing on a test server if required.

### Review Progress

#### Performance Review

- [x] I (as a reviewer) confirm that the client changes are implemented with a very good performance even for very large courses with more than 2000 students.

#### Code Review

- [x] Code Review 1
- [x] Code Review 2

#### Manual Tests

- [x] Test 1
- [x] Test 2

### Test Coverage
<!-- Please add the test coverages for all changed files modified in this PR here. You can generate the coverage table using one of these options: -->
<!-- 1. Run `pnpm run coverage:pr` to generate coverage locally by running only the affected module tests (see supporting_scripts/code-coverage/local-pr-coverage/README.md) -->
<!-- 2. Use `supporting_scripts/code-coverage/generate_code_cov_table/generate_code_cov_table.py` to generate the table from CI artifacts (requires GitHub token, follow the README for setup) -->
<!-- The line coverage must be above 90% for changed files, and you must use extensive and useful assertions for server tests and expect statements for client tests. -->
<!-- Note: Confirm in the last column that you have implemented extensive assertions for server tests and expect statements for client tests. -->
<!--       Remove rows with only trivial changes from the table. -->

**Warning:** Coverage table could not be generated. The `Test` workflow concluded with status `failure`; coverage artifacts may be missing or incomplete. See [workflow logs](https://github.com/ls1intum/Artemis/actions/runs/26245235765).

_Last updated: 2026-05-21 19:08:00 UTC_

Tested on TS1. Worked as expected. LGTM

Tested the changes on TS2: Created a new Apollon D&D quiz exercise and added 2 classes with a relationship; If nothing is selected in 'Select Element' mode the 'Generate a Quiz Exercise' button is grayed out: After selecting a class, relationship and attribute the button becomes available:

The assessment fix works, the quiz feature too. Great work! I will try to integrate this quickly in the PR so we can merge this without a broken teams feature.

Verified that collaboration mode works with the new incremental updates on TS3. Remaining parts also worked. I briefly looked over the code and it generally looks good to me. The `@Output` introduced will be removed in a follow-up eventually. This is fine for now as this PR includes a critical fix for the assessment popover

technicalSummary: Supports nested Apollon selections in Artemis quiz generation, including class attributes/methods, accurate cutouts, and Apollon dependency update.

body: ### Summary This PR updates the Apollon quiz import flow so Artemis explicitly opts into Apollon quiz mode, correctly derives quiz-relevant elements from the editor model, and generates drag-and-drop questions with properly aligned backgrounds, drop locations, and drag item images. ### Checklist #### General - [x] I tested **all** changes and their related features with **all** corresponding user types on a test server. - [x] Language: I followed the [guidelines for inclusive, diversity-sensitive, and appreciative language](https://docs.artemis.tum.de/developer/guidelines/language). - [x] I chose a title conforming to the [naming conventions for pull requests](https://docs.artemis.tum.de/developer/development-process#pr-naming-conventions). #### Client - [x] **Important**: I implemented the changes with a very good performance, prevented too many (unnecessary) REST calls and made sure the UI is responsive, even with large data (e.g. using paging). - [x] I **strictly** followed the principle of **data economy** for all client-server REST calls. - [x] I **strictly** followed the [client coding guidelines](https://docs.artemis.tum.de/developer/guidelines/client-development). - [x] I **strictly** followed the [AET UI-UX guidelines](https://ls1intum.github.io/ui-ux-guidelines/). - [x] Following the [theming guidelines](https://docs.artemis.tum.de/developer/guidelines/client-theming), I specified colors only in the theming variable files and checked that the changes look consistent in both the light and the dark theme. - [x] I added multiple integration tests (Vitest) related to the features (with a high test coverage), while following the [test guidelines](https://docs.artemis.tum.de/developer/guidelines/client-tests). - [x] I added `authorities` to all new routes and checked the course groups for displaying navigation elements (links, buttons). - [x] I documented the TypeScript code using JSDoc style. - [x] I added multiple screenshots/screencasts of my UI changes. - [x] I translated all newly inserted strings into English and German. ### Motivation and Context Generating drag-and-drop quiz questions from Apollon diagrams was unreliable in the Artemis quiz flow. The main issues were: - Artemis did not explicitly opt into the new Apollon quiz mode. - The generate button could stay disabled even when quiz-relevant elements existed. - Generated drag items and drop locations could be oversized because single-element SVG exports contained extra internal padding. - The drop-location overlay could render before the generated background image was fully ready, leading to incorrect initial positioning. - Background generation needed to preserve the full diagram while removing only the selected quiz elements. ### Description This PR updates the Artemis integration with Apollon for drag-and-drop quiz generation: - Explicitly enables Apollon quiz mode in the quiz diagram detail flow using `enableQuizMode: true`. - Uses `importDiagram(...)` when loading stored Apollon diagrams in the quiz dialog. - Replaces the brittle quiz-relevance predicate with shared helper logic based on `model.interactive`, with fallback to model elements when needed. - Generates the quiz background from the full diagram export and blanks out selected drop areas afterward. - Trims per-element SVG exports before converting them to PNGs, so drag items and computed drop locations match the visible element content more closely. - Uses the trimmed element clip for drop-location computation. - Defers rendering of drag-and-drop overlays until the generated/imported background image is fully loaded. - Uses `svgMode: 'compat'` for quiz-related SVG exports to preserve correct colors in generated images. ### Steps for Testing Prerequisites: - 1 Instructor - 1 Course - 1 Quiz Exercise - 1 Apollon diagram with at least two nodes and one edge - Enable the `ApollonQuizDragAndDrop` feature toggle if required in the test environment 1. Log in to Artemis as instructor. 2. Navigate to a quiz exercise and open the Apollon import dialog. 3. Open an existing Apollon diagram in the quiz detail view. 4. Verify the editor is editable and that quiz mode controls are available in this flow. 5. Model a diagram or use an existing one with at least one node and one edge. 6. Mark one or more quiz-relevant elements in Apollon. 7. Verify that `Generate Quiz Exercise` becomes enabled. 8. Generate the drag-and-drop question. 9. Verify that the generated background keeps the full diagram and removes only the selected elements. 10. Verify that generated drag item images are tightly cropped and no longer contain large white padding. 11. Verify that the generated drop locations align with the removed areas in the background. 12. Open the generated drag-and-drop question in edit/preview mode. 13. Verify that the overlay is positioned correctly after the background image loads. 14. Verify that colors are correct in both light and dark theme. #### Exam Mode Testing Prerequisites: - 1 Instructor - 1 Exam with a Quiz Exercise containing an Apollon-generated drag-and-drop question 1. Log in as instructor and open the exam. 2. Start the exam as a student. 3. Open the generated drag-and-drop question. 4. Verify that the background image, drop locations, and drag items render correctly in exam mode as well. 5. Verify that the UI remains usable and visually unchanged apart from the intended fix. ### Review Progress #### Code Review - [x] Code Review 1 - [x] Code Review 2 #### Manual Tests - [x] Test 1 - [x] Test 2 ### Screenshots - Add screenshots or a short screencast showing: - Creating quiz with Apollon Drag and Drop option (intructor view) - Automatically generated drop locations and options (instructor view) - Quiz participation (student view) - Quiz submission (student) ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Flexible quiz/interactivity-aware element selection (explicit flags or fallback to all). * SVG trimming utility to extract element content and improved SVG export compatibility. * Editor now tracks an imported model snapshot for accurate save-state detection. * Background-ready flag to gate background-dependent UI and interactions. * **Bug Fixes** * Background generation blanks drop areas and manages image/canvas lifecycle more robustly. * Drop-location placement stabilized using trimmed SVG clip bounds. * Export and SVG generation use a more compatible mode for consistent outputs. * **Tests** * Added per-test canvas/image mocks with proper setup/teardown for reliable image/export tests. * **Chores** * Updated Apollon dependency to a newer patch version.
title: `Quiz exercises`: Fix apollon drag-and-drop quiz generation

I think with the selection issue I just confused myself, seems to be a usability issue since the colors are so close between modeling and selection, we can just merge it as is and do the remaining improvements as follow-up

technicalSummary: Fixes Apollon drag-and-drop quiz generation by enabling the correct editor mode, import path, SVG rendering, background generation, and drop-area alignment.

body:
<!-- Thanks for contributing to Artemis! Before you submit your pull request, please make sure to check all tasks by putting an x in the [ ] (don't: [x ], [ x], do: [x]). Remove not applicable tasks and do not leave them unchecked -->
<!-- If your pull request is not ready for review yet, create a draft pull request! -->

### Checklist
#### General
- [x] I tested **all** changes and their related features with **all** corresponding user types on a test server.
- [x] Language: I followed the [guidelines for inclusive, diversity-sensitive, and appreciative language](https://docs.artemis.cit.tum.de/dev/guidelines/language-guidelines/).
- [x] I chose a title conforming to the [naming conventions for pull requests](https://docs.artemis.cit.tum.de/dev/development-process/development-process.html#naming-conventions-for-github-pull-requests).

#### Client
- [x] **Important**: I implemented the changes with a very good performance, prevented too many (unnecessary) REST calls and made sure the UI is responsive, even with large data (e.g. using paging).
- [x] I **strictly** followed the principle of **data economy** for all client-server REST calls.
- [x] I **strictly** followed the [client coding guidelines](https://docs.artemis.cit.tum.de/dev/guidelines/client/).
- [x] I **strictly** followed the [AET UI-UX guidelines](https://ls1intum.github.io/ui-ux-guidelines/).
- [x] Following the [theming guidelines](https://docs.artemis.cit.tum.de/dev/guidelines/client-design/), I specified colors only in the theming variable files and checked that the changes look consistent in both the light and the dark theme.
- [x] I added multiple integration tests (Jest) related to the features (with a high test coverage), while following the [test guidelines](https://docs.artemis.cit.tum.de/dev/guidelines/client-tests/).
- [x] I documented the TypeScript code using JSDoc style.

#### Changes affecting Modeling Exercises
- [x] **High priority**: I tested **all** changes and their related features with **all** corresponding user types on a test server configured with the **integrated lifecycle setup** (LocalVC and LocalCI).
- [x] I tested **all** changes and their related features with **all** corresponding user types on a test server configured with **LocalVC** and **Jenkins**.


### Motivation and Context
Upgrade from the legacy Apollon library (`@ls1intum/apollon` v3) to the new Apollon v2 library (`@tumaet/apollon` v4) which provides an improved diagram editor with better performance and maintainability.

### Description
This PR upgrades the Apollon diagram editor library and adapts all modeling-related components to the new API:

**Dependency Changes:**
- Replaced `@ls1intum/apollon` (v3.4.6) with `@tumaet/apollon` (v4.2.11)
- Removed `fast-json-patch` (no longer needed with new sync mechanism)

**API Migration:**
- Model subscription: `subscribeToModelChangePatches(callback)` → `sendBroadcastMessage(callback)`
- Unsubscribe: `unsubscribeFromModelChange`/`unsubscribeFromModelChangePatches` → `unsubscribe`
- Patch type: `Patch` object → `string` (base64 encoded)
- Selection API: `selectedEntities`/`selectedRelationships` → `selectedElementIds`
- Model import: Added `importDiagram()` for converting serialized models
- Assessment names: `findElement`/`findRelationship` with manual type mapping → `getAssessmentNameForArtemis()`

**New Files:**
- `apollon-model.util.ts`: Utility functions for format-agnostic model access (`getModelNodes`, `getModelEdges`, `countModelElements`, `isModelEmpty`, `hasModelElements`, `getModelElementIds`)
- `apollon-model.util.spec.ts`: Comprehensive test suite for utility functions

**Backward Compatibility:**
- Full support for existing v3 models (elements/relationships objects) alongside new v4 format (nodes/edges arrays)
- The utility module handles format detection transparently

**Component Updates:**
- `modeling-editor.component.ts`: Updated editor initialization and event handling
- `modeling-submission.component.ts`: Updated model validation and change detection
- `modeling-assessment.component.ts`: Simplified assessment name resolution
- `modeling-assessment.util.ts`: Replaced manual type mapping with library function
- `team-submission-sync.component.ts`: Updated for new patch format
- `apollon-diagram-detail.component.ts`: Updated diagram management
- `quiz-exercise-generator.ts`: Updated quiz generation from diagrams

### Steps for Testing
Prerequisites:

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Please comment which parts of the modeling exercises you have tested and with which browser! Partial manual testing is also welcome!**
>
> **We need to test the flow with all diagram types!**
> - Class diagram
> - Object diagram
> - Component diagram
> - Deployment diagram
> - Flowchart
> - Syntax tree
> - Sequential function chart diagram
> - Activity diagram
> - Use case diagram
> - Communication diagram
> - Petri net
> - Reachability graph
> - BPMN diagram
>
> You can also compare https://apollon.aet.cit.tum.de with https://apollon2.aet.cit.tum.de when it is about testing the diagram editing capabilities.

**Modeling Exercise Creation**
Prerequisites: 
- 1 Instructor

1. Log in to Artemis
2. Navigate to a Course
3. Navigate to Exercises -> Create Modeling Exercise
4. The Apollon2 editor should render and be functional as expected.
5. Fill out the required forms and click generate.

**Assessment**
Prerequisites: 
- Modeling exercise, ideally class diagram (with structured grading instructions, i.e. drag and drop feedback templates)
- Modeling exercise submission by student
- 1 Instructor
- 1 Student

1. Open the Assessment Dashboard of the modeling exercise
2. Place feedback on the submission
   2.1 (Double) click on a UML element and give feedback
   2.2 Drag and drop feedback on a element and/or a nested element, i.e. class diagram attribute or method
3. Submit
4. View feedback as student and verify that it is correct

**Testing backward compatibility with v3 or older versions**
Prerequisites: 
- Old modeling exercise created using a old Artemis version

As instructor:
1. On the Course Management site
5. Press "Edit" on any existing V3 model from the modeling exercises list.
6. Verify that you can edit and save the 
7. Import an old modeling exercise and try if it also works

As student:
1. Participate in a modeling exercise that has been created previously
2. You should be able to submit as usual
3. Ideally: Also test with a exercise where you have previously submitted using the old format on a older version of Artemis

Assessment:
1. Open and edit an assessment of a old modeling exercise (submitted using an older version of Artemis)
2. You should be able to assess as usual

**Request Feedback with Athena**
> [!WARNING]
> Only works on an Athena enabled test server TS1, TS2, TS3 

Prerequisite:
- Modeling exercise enabled with automatic feedback requests (Athena)
- 1 Student

1. Submit modeling submission
2. Press "Request Feedback" 
3. Feedback request should work (but there needs to be feedback generated, which is not deterministic)

### Testserver States
You can manage test servers using [Helios](https://helios.aet.cit.tum.de/). Check environment statuses in the [environment list](https://helios.aet.cit.tum.de/repo/69562331/environment/list). To deploy to a test server, go to the [CI/CD](https://helios.aet.cit.tum.de/repo/69562331/ci-cd) page, find your PR or branch, and trigger the deployment.

#### Code Review
- [x] Code Review 1
- [x] Code Review 2
#### Manual Tests
 - [x] Main flow (create, edit, participate, request feedback, manual assessment, see assement)
   - [x] Manual test (Chrome)

_Last updated: 2026-04-02 15:03:05 UTC_


### Screenshots
Apollon editor UI is mostly unchanged - we are now using the improved editor from https://apollon2.aet.cit.tum.de/

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
  * Switched to a new Apollon diagram library.

* **New Features**
  * Unified selection model exposed as selectedElementIds.
  * Initial collaborative patch sync emitted on startup.

* **Improvements**
  * Transparent support for legacy (v3) and new (v4) diagram formats with normalization utilities.
  * Editor/layout tweaks, fixed editor sizing, and a close button for the help modal.
  * More robust import and validation for example and submission diagrams.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * More reliable loading, saving and assessment flows across diagram formats.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

<details>
<summary>src/main/webapp/app/quiz/manage/apollon-diagrams/detail/apollon-diagram-detail.component.ts (1)</summary><blockquote>

`70-82`: **Avoid `any` in model-shape checks; use typed narrowing.**

`Line 70` drops type safety with `as any`, making v3/v4 handling brittle and harder to validate at compile time. Please replace this with explicit type guards (or shared typed helpers) for interactive/nodes/edges shape checks

Tested on TS6 during testing session. I tested creating a syntax tree exercise in Safari and participating+assessing it. It works in general, but there is a visual issue when viewing assessments: When navigating to the graded result, **feedback icons** (checkmark, exclamation point, cross) are only shown inline on connections between terminals/non-terminals, not the boxes themselves. These **only show up after clicking** on the boxes. Screenshot before clicking on an item:
I have tested on TS6 with BPMN diagram but the feed back from instructor couldnt show when the student review the submission results and the left rand of the whole diagram could not show fully

Tested on ts6 during the testing session. Creating a diagram, assessing it and viewing the assessment worked for me but when i tried to export the solution I got an error message Additionally, this might not be related to the PR but I was able to drag & drop a grading instruction as many time as I wanted to, even though limit was set to 1 (See Class 1 grading

Tested on ts6 in testing session. I tested class diagrams in exam mode.

Interestingly, it worked well for the first but not for the second modeling exercise. As you can see in the screen recordings, the associations are displayed correctly in the first but not in the second exercise. (Tested using Firefox).

First exercise working:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/abea65fd-e42e-4f4d-a755-69a68ec041d5

Second exercise not showing associations correctly:
https://github.com/user-

Tested following things during the testing session on TS6:
- Modeling Exercise Creation
- Assessment
- Testing backward compatibility with v3 or older versions (except step 3.)

The only thing I noticed is that I was able to apply a feedback via drag and drop more times than the limit:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5d9ee8ff-53c6-4eab-b651-dabda57d46c6

Tested on TS4, the problem I found previously seems to be expected

Was only able to test class diagrams because I could not figure out how to create different types of diagrams in modeling exercises
reapprove

Tested on TS3. My previous comment was addressed, the icons for feedback show up immediately. However, I noticed two other issues: Sample solutions are not rendered correctly (text is highlighted to show that the text exists, just in the wrong color): Whenever I open the assessment of my solution, I get this message showing up (every single time, despite nothing changing). Not sure if
Tested exercise creation and feedback for class diagrams, object diagrams, deployment diagrams, and flowcharts on Firefox, works as expected.

I tested editing syntax tree, sequential function chart, BPMN, activity diagram, use case diagram, and petri net on TS3. I noticed this niche behavior when just clicking nodes on the same element consecutively: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/69cc9174-c52e-4e1d-b256-c1de5e16e69c I am not sure whether this is intended or not but I cannot connect the notes elements to anything as they do not show any blue connection points:

We did some dependency updates in the library to resolve some security issues but it still works as expected for me. Since this has been now extensively tested with prior approvals I will merge it. 

Thanks @egenerse, @kurunbelemir, @tamang29, and @FadyGergesRezk for the contribution! I will also switch over the standalone deployment today and migrate to the Apollon repo with the Apollon2 monorepo.

featureCategory: Apollon v3 to v4 migration support
technicalSummary: Contributed commits to the Apollon v3 to v4 migration in Artemis, including compatibility tests, package maintenance, and the SGI assessment scoring fix for flat Apollon dropInfo serialization.

body:
<!-- Thanks for contributing to Artemis! Before you submit your pull request, please make sure to check all tasks by putting an x in the [ ] (don't: [x ], [ x], do: [x]). Remove not applicable tasks and do not leave them unchecked -->
<!-- If your pull request is not ready for review yet, create a draft pull request! -->

### Checklist
#### General
- [x] I tested **all** changes and their related features with **all** corresponding user types on a test server.
- [x] Language: I followed the [guidelines for inclusive, diversity-sensitive, and appreciative language](https://docs.artemis.cit.tum.de/dev/guidelines/language-guidelines/).
- [x] I chose a title conforming to the [naming conventions for pull requests](https://docs.artemis.cit.tum.de/dev/development-process/development-process.html#naming-conventions-for-github-pull-requests).

#### Client
- [x] **Important**: I implemented the changes with a very good performance, prevented too many (unnecessary) REST calls and made sure the UI is responsive, even with large data (e.g. using paging).
- [x] I **strictly** followed the principle of **data economy** for all client-server REST calls.
- [x] I **strictly** followed the [client coding guidelines](https://docs.artemis.cit.tum.de/dev/guidelines/client/).
- [x] I **strictly** followed the [AET UI-UX guidelines](https://ls1intum.github.io/ui-ux-guidelines/).
- [x] Following the [theming guidelines](https://docs.artemis.cit.tum.de/dev/guidelines/client-design/), I specified colors only in the theming variable files and checked that the changes look consistent in both the light and the dark theme.
- [x] I added multiple integration tests (Jest) related to the features (with a high test coverage), while following the [test guidelines](https://docs.artemis.cit.tum.de/dev/guidelines/client-tests/).
- [x] I documented the TypeScript code using JSDoc style.

#### Changes affecting Modeling Exercises
- [x] **High priority**: I tested **all** changes and their related features with **all** corresponding user types on a test server configured with the **integrated lifecycle setup** (LocalVC and LocalCI).
- [x] I tested **all** changes and their related features with **all** corresponding user types on a test server configured with **LocalVC** and **Jenkins**.


### Motivation and Context
Upgrade from the legacy Apollon library (`@ls1intum/apollon` v3) to the new Apollon v2 library (`@tumaet/apollon` v4) which provides an improved diagram editor with better performance and maintainability.

### Description
This PR upgrades the Apollon diagram editor library and adapts all modeling-related components to the new API:

**Dependency Changes:**
- Replaced `@ls1intum/apollon` (v3.4.6) with `@tumaet/apollon` (v4.2.6)
- Removed `fast-json-patch` (no longer needed with new sync mechanism)

**API Migration:**
- Model subscription: `subscribeToModelChangePatches(callback)` → `sendBroadcastMessage(callback)`
- Unsubscribe: `unsubscribeFromModelChange`/`unsubscribeFromModelChangePatches` → `unsubscribe`
- Patch type: `Patch` object → `string` (base64 encoded)
- Selection API: `selectedEntities`/`selectedRelationships` → `selectedElementIds`
- Model import: Added `importDiagram()` for converting serialized models
- Assessment names: `findElement`/`findRelationship` with manual type mapping → `getAssessmentNameForArtemis()`

**New Files:**
- `apollon-model.util.ts`: Utility functions for format-agnostic model access (`getModelNodes`, `getModelEdges`, `countModelElements`, `isModelEmpty`, `hasModelElements`, `getModelElementIds`)
- `apollon-model.util.spec.ts`: Comprehensive test suite for utility functions

**Backward Compatibility:**
- Full support for existing v3 models (elements/relationships objects) alongside new v4 format (nodes/edges arrays)
- The utility module handles format detection transparently

**Component Updates:**
- `modeling-editor.component.ts`: Updated editor initialization and event handling
- `modeling-submission.component.ts`: Updated model validation and change detection
- `modeling-assessment.component.ts`: Simplified assessment name resolution
- `modeling-assessment.util.ts`: Replaced manual type mapping with library function
- `team-submission-sync.component.ts`: Updated for new patch format
- `apollon-diagram-detail.component.ts`: Updated diagram management
- `quiz-exercise-generator.ts`: Updated quiz generation from diagrams

### Steps for Testing
Prerequisites:

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Please comment which parts of the modeling exercises you have tested and with which browser! Partial manual testing is also welcome!**
>
> **We need to test the flow with all diagram types!**
> - Class diagram
> - Object diagram
> - Component diagram
> - Deployment diagram
> - Flowchart
> - Syntax tree
> - Sequential function chart diagram
> - Activity diagram
> - Use case diagram
> - Communication diagram
> - Petri net
> - Reachability graph
> - BPMN diagram
>
> You can also compare https://apollon.aet.cit.tum.de with https://apollon2.aet.cit.tum.de when it is about testing the diagram editing capabilities.

**Modeling Exercise Creation**
Prerequisites: 
- 1 Instructor

1. Log in to Artemis
2. Navigate to a Course
3. Navigate to Exercises -> Create Modeling Exercise
4. The Apollon2 editor should render and be functional as expected.
5. Fill out the required forms and click generate.

**Assessment**
Prerequisites: 
- Modeling exercise, ideally class diagram (with structured grading instructions, i.e. drag and drop feedback templates)
- Modeling exercise submission by student
- 1 Instructor
- 1 Student

1. Open the Assessment Dashboard of the modeling exercise
2. Place feedback on the submission
   2.1 (Double) click on a UML element and give feedback
   2.2 Drag and drop feedback on a element and/or a nested element, i.e. class diagram attribute or method
3. Submit
4. View feedback as student and verify that it is correct

**Testing backward compatibility with v3 or older versions**
Prerequisites: 
- Old modeling exercise created using a old Artemis version

As instructor:
1. On the Course Management site
5. Press "Edit" on any existing V3 model from the modeling exercises list.
6. Verify that you can edit and save the 
7. Import an old modeling exercise and try if it also works

As student:
1. Participate in a modeling exercise that has been created previously
2. You should be able to submit as usual
3. Ideally: Also test with a exercise where you have previously submitted using the old format on a older version of Artemis

Assessment:
1. Open and edit an assessment of a old modeling exercise (submitted using an older version of Artemis)
2. You should be able to assess as usual

**Request Feedback with Athena**
> [!WARNING]
> Only works on an Athena enabled test server TS1, TS2, TS3 

Prerequisite:
- Modeling exercise enabled with automatic feedback requests (Athena)
- 1 Student

1. Submit modeling submission
2. Press "Request Feedback" 
3. Feedback request should work (but there needs to be feedback generated, which is not deterministic)

### Testserver States
You can manage test servers using [Helios](https://helios.aet.cit.tum.de/). Check environment statuses in the [environment list](https://helios.aet.cit.tum.de/repo/69562331/environment/list). To deploy to a test server, go to the [CI/CD](https://helios.aet.cit.tum.de/repo/69562331/ci-cd) page, find your PR or branch, and trigger the deployment.

#### Code Review
- [x] Code Review 1
- [ ] Code Review 2
#### Manual Tests
 - [x] Main flow (create, edit, participate, request feedback, manual assessment, see assement)
   - [x] Manual test (Chrome)
   - [x] Manual test (Safari)
   - [ ] Manual test (Firefox)
 - [x] Participating in team exercises
 - [x] Backwards compatibility
 - [ ] Testing the diagram types
   - [x] Class diagram
     - [x] Manual test (Chrome)
     - [x] Manual test (Safari)
     - [ ] Manual test (Firefox)
   - [x] Object diagram
     - [x] Manual test (Chrome)
     - [x] Manual test (Safari)
     - [ ] Manual test (Firefox)
   - [x] Component diagram
     - [x] Manual test (Chrome)
     - [ ] Manual test (Safari)
     - [x] Manual test (Firefox)
   - [ ] Deployment diagram
     - [ ] Manual test (Chrome)
     - [ ] Manual test (Safari)
     - [ ] Manual test (Firefox)
   - [x] Flowchart
     - [x] Manual test (Chrome)
     - [ ] Manual test (Safari)
     - [ ] Manual test (Firefox)
   - [ ] Syntax tree
     - [ ] Manual test (Chrome)
     - [ ] Manual test (Safari)
     - [ ] Manual test (Firefox)
   - [ ] Sequential function chart diagram
     - [ ] Manual test (Chrome)
     - [ ] Manual test (Safari)
     - [ ] Manual test (Firefox)
   - [x] Activity diagram
     - [x] Manual test (Chrome)
     - [ ] Manual test (Safari)
     - [ ] Manual test (Firefox)
   - [x] Use case diagram
     - [x] Manual test (Chrome)
     - [ ] Manual test (Safari)
     - [x] Manual test (Firefox)
   - [ ] Communication diagram
     - [ ] Manual test (Chrome)
     - [ ] Manual test (Safari)
     - [x] Manual test (Firefox)
   - [x] Petri net
     - [x] Manual test (Chrome)
     - [ ] Manual test (Safari)
     - [ ] Manual test (Firefox)
   - [ ] Reachability graph
     - [ ] Manual test (Chrome)
     - [ ] Manual test (Safari)
     - [ ] Manual test (Firefox)
   - [x] BPMN diagram
     - [x] Manual test (Chrome)
     - [ ] Manual test (Safari)
     - [ ] Manual test (Firefox)

### Test Coverage
<!-- Please add the test coverages for all changed files modified in this PR here. You can generate the coverage table using one of these options: -->
<!-- 1. Run `npm run coverage:pr` to generate coverage locally by running only the affected module tests (see supporting_scripts/code-coverage/local-pr-coverage/README.md) -->
<!-- 2. Use `supporting_scripts/code-coverage/generate_code_cov_table/generate_code_cov_table.py` to generate the table from CI artifacts (requires GitHub token, follow the README for setup) -->
<!-- The line coverage must be above 90% for changed files, and you must use extensive and useful assertions for server tests and expect statements for client tests. -->
<!-- Note: Confirm in the last column that you have implemented extensive assertions for server tests and expect statements for client tests. -->
<!--       Remove rows with only trivial changes from the table. -->

**Warning:** Client tests failed. Coverage could not be fully measured. Please check the [workflow logs](https://github.com/ls1intum/Artemis/actions/runs/21394136165).

#### Server

| Class/File | Line Coverage | Lines |
|------------|-------------:|------:|
| ModelingSubmission.java | 84.62% | 75 |

_Last updated: 2026-01-27 11:12:20 UTC_


### Screenshots
Apollon editor UI is mostly unchanged - we are now using the improved editor from https://apollon2.aet.cit.tum.de/

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Editor now supports the newer node/edge diagram format, emits refined selection events, adds a Close button in help, and sends an initial sync for team collaboration.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Improved compatibility across diagram versions with more reliable parsing, highlighting, validation, export, and collaboration flows.

* **Tests**
  * Expanded and adapted tests across modeling, assessment, submission, quiz, and editor workflows for v3/v4 formats.

* **Chores**
  * Switched to an updated diagram library, added its stylesheet, and updated package references and patch payload handling.

<sub>✏️ Tip: You can customize this high-level summary in your review settings.</sub>
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
